channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Disorder Breaks Out After Belfast Knife Attack
item.description: People took to the streets of Belfast on Tuesday after a suspect, who the police said was a 30-year-old Sudanese man, was charged with attempted murder over a knife attack.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Press Association, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Vehicles set on fire by protesters in east Belfast on Tuesday.

item.title: ¿Por qué bajan las tasas de natalidad? Los celulares podrían ser responsables
item.description: Los celulares inteligentes salieron al mercado en 2007, el año en que las tasas de fertilidad empezaron a bajar. Dos estudios dicen que no es una coincidencia.
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Tavernise

media:content.media:credit: Colin Clark para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: La fertilidad no solo está bajando en Estados Unidos. El misterio es ahora un fenómeno casi global.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes in New Test of Cease-Fire
item.description: The U.S. military struck southern Iran, calling it a response to the downing an American helicopter. Iran said it had retaliated by launching drones and missiles at U.S. military targets in Bahrain and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Vessels anchored in the Strait of Hormuz near Musandam, Oman.

item.title: Graham Platner Wins Maine Senate Primary
item.description: Graham Platner, a progressive oyster farmer, won the Democratic nomination for Senate on Tuesday. He is set to face Senator Susan Collins, a five-term Republican, in November.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner in Blue Hill, Maine, after winning the state’s Democratic primary for Senate on Tuesday.

item.title: The City That Watches Together
item.description: In bodegas and parks, in bars and pizzerias, the unofficial watch party has taken over New York.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein and The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 10, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Linsanity Is Dead. Long Live Linsanity.
item.description: Knicks legend Jeremy Lin on the Finals, the Garden and Kim Kardashian.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Torre, Walter Avaroma, Maxwell Carney, Ryan Cortes, Juan Galindo, Patrick Kim, Neely Lohmann, Rob McRae, Clare Taylor, Chris Tumminello and Matt Sullivan

item.title: Tokio tiene una nueva policía contra la basura
item.description: En Shibuya, una de las zonas más turísticas y concurridas de Tokio, tirar basura ahora tiene un precio.
item.dc:creator: Kiuko Notoya

media:content.media:credit: Kiuko Notoya/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: On the streets of Shibuya, patrol teams caught 10 people littering on the first day of the new ordinance, 15 on the second day and 9 on the third, according to city officials.

item.title: Word of the Day: effete
item.description: This word has appeared in four articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Calls a Technical Foul on Trump’s Apparent Game 3 Nap
item.description: The late night host called Trump “the first sitting president to shut down a major United States city so he could take a nap in front of a sold-out crowd at the N.B.A. finals.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Maine and South Carolina
item.description: Graham Platner won the Democratic primary for Senate in Maine and quickly turned his populist message against Susan Collins, the Republican incumbent.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner in Blue Hill, Maine, after winning the state’s Democratic primary for Senate on Tuesday.

item.title: The Global Bull Market That A.I. Obscures
item.description: The rest of the world has been writing one of the more remarkable chapters in modern financial history — and many have hardly noticed.
item.dc:creator: Liaquat Ahamed

media:content.media:credit: Max Slobodda for The New York Times

item.title: Los riesgos del alcohol en la salud aumentan tras una sola copa al día
item.description: La industria del alcohol ha criticado el estudio que reveló el dato y que también señala que un consumo moderado de alcohol aumenta el riesgo de muerte prematura.
item.dc:creator: Roni Caryn Rabin
item.category: Journal of Studies on Alcohol and Drugs

media:content.media:credit: Cedric Angeles para The New York Times

item.title: Republicans in Maine Governor’s Race Head to Ranked-Choice Runoff
item.description: State election officials will confirm the winner in the next week or two, after a counting process that involves multiple rounds of elimination.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters in Augusta, Maine, filling out their ballots on Tuesday.

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Mayor’s Line in the Sand to Be a Host: FIFA’s Request for a Soldier Field Dome
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, June 10, 2026.

item.title: Democrats in Maine Governor’s Race Head to Ranked-Choice Runoff
item.description: State election officials will confirm the winner in the coming weeks, after a counting process that involves multiple rounds of elimination.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell

media:content.media:credit: Associated Press and Reuters

item.title: Corrections: June 10, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, June 10, 2026.

item.title: Democrats Face Another Long Wait for Results in Maine Swing District
item.description: No candidate cleared 50 percent in the Democratic primary race to replace Representative Jared Golden, sending the race into ranked-choice voting.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner and Tim Balk

item.title: Nevada Attorney General Wins Democratic Nomination for Governor
item.description: Aaron Ford easily outpaced a Washoe County commissioner, Alexis Hill, for the nod to take on Gov. Joe Lombardo in November.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault

media:content.media:credit: Jae C. Hong/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Attorney General Aaron Ford of Nevada celebrating in Las Vegas after winning the Democratic nomination for governor on Tuesday.

item.title: Republican Primary for Open Congressional Seat in South Carolina Heads to Runoff
item.description: The largely coastal district has voted overwhelmingly Republican in recent elections. Representative Nancy Mace ran unsuccessfully for governor instead of seeking re-election to the seat.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Republican primary for South Carolina’s First Congressional District advanced to a runoff on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump’s Sharp Turn on China: Embracing It as a Peer Power
item.description: President Trump’s warming with Xi Jinping of China, a leader he admires, has ignited anxieties in Washington and across Asia.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The U.S. and Chinese governments rolled out a new phrase, “constructive strategic stability,” during President Trump’s meeting in Beijing last month with the leader of China, Xi Jinping.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 10, 2026
item.description: David J. Kahn and Ethan Quigley kick off their crossword collaboration for The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Matthew Roth for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Hint: The sport featured in this image isn’t the one you’re looking for.

item.title: Is the U.S.-Israel Alliance Still Strategic?
item.description: Is the U.S.-Israel alliance doing more harm than good? Matt Duss, the executive vice president at the Center for International Policy, joins “The Ezra Klein Show” to discuss whether the strategic and moral costs of the U.S.-Israel alliance outweigh the benefits.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Trump Says Bill Pulte Will Take Over as Intelligence Chief on June 19
item.description: The president also plans to keep Mr. Pulte as the head of a federal housing agency, heightening tension with Congress and complicating the renewal of a powerful spying authority.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump has suggested that Bill Pulte might use the temporary appointment as director of national intelligence as an opportunity to purge the office of perceived political opponents.

item.title: Platner Wins Maine Senate Primary After a Turbulent Stretch of His Bid
item.description: Graham Platner has faced new scrutiny over his personal history, but his Democratic primary victory sets up a high-stakes race against Senator Susan Collins, a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner’s primary victory was long expected after his main Democratic opponent, Gov. Janet Mills, suspended her campaign in late April.

item.title: Evette and Wilson Advance to Runoff in South Carolina Republican Primary for Governor
item.description: Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette and Alan Wilson, the state attorney general, now head to a runoff in the Republican contest for governor. President Trump vowed to “fight” for Ms. Evette in the second round.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina, Emily Cochrane and Nick Madigan

media:content.media:credit: Meg Kinnard/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette of South Carolina at a campaign rally on Monday.

item.title: Sanders-Backed Progressive Advances in California Swing District Against Valadao
item.description: Randy Villegas will face the incumbent, David Valadao, a Republican who has survived numerous challenges in a competitive district.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Jon Kopaloff/Getty Images For Connor Treacy
media:content.media:description: Randy Villegas, a progressive backed by Senator Bernie Sanders of Vermont, defeated Dr. Jasmeet Bains in the Democratic primary in California’s 22nd Congressional District. He will face Representative David Valadao, the Republican incumbent.

item.title: Lindsey Graham Wins Republican Senate Primary in South Carolina
item.description: Mr. Graham, a four-term incumbent, is the favorite to win the general election in a solidly Republican state.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham, Republican of South Carolina, in Washington in February.

item.title: Navy Admiral Fired by Hegseth Advances to Runoff to Replace Mace
item.description: Nancy Lacore would face a tough fall election in a district drawn in 2021 to be more Republican. She is seeking to replace Representative Nancy Mace, who ran unsuccessfully for governor instead of running for re-election.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Shawn Thew/EPA, via Shutterstock
media:content.media:description: Nancy Lacore, a former U.S. Navy admiral, is seeking a congressional seat in South Carolina after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth fired her last year.

item.title: Majority of City Council Members Urge Mamdani to Pause A.I. in Schools
item.description: The demand from 29 members comes as the Department of Education is updating its guidance for how teachers should use the technology in their work and classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Council Member Alexa Avilés joined an April rally supporting a proposed moratorium on artificial intelligence in New York City classrooms.

item.title: Trump ordena atacar Irán por el derribo de un helicóptero de EE. UU.
item.description: En un comunicado, el Comando Central afirmó que los ataques de “autodefensa”, llevados a cabo por orden del presidente fueron “una respuesta proporcional a la agresión iraní injustificada”.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Maggie Haberman and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times

item.title: Southern Baptists Elect Willy Rice, Pastor Favored by Right-Wing Faction, as President
item.description: A contingent seeking to push the denomination to the right secured its first major victory through the election of Willy Rice, a Florida pastor who wants to challenge “the system.”
item.dc:creator: Ruth Graham

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Willy Rice, a Florida pastor, at the Southern Baptist Convention’s annual meeting in Dallas last year. On Tuesday, he won this year’s presidential election.

item.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley, Independent, to Face Richard Pan in California House Race
item.description: Mr. Kiley dropped his Republican affiliation earlier this year. The newly drawn Sixth Congressional District is widely considered to be favorable for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times, Rich Pedroncelli/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Representative Kevin Kiley and Richard Pan will compete in November.

item.title: Dallas Judge Blocks City Council Vote on Future of City Hall
item.description: The ruling gives officials more time to weigh competing plans for the future of Dallas’s aging City Hall building and the land it occupies.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dallas city leaders are considering a proposal to abandon City Hall, designed by the architect I.M. Pei, raising the possibility that the building could be demolished.

item.title: Kalshi Will Ask Users to Share Employer Information for Some Trades
item.description: The new policy is aimed at limiting insider trading as scrutiny of prediction markets grows.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McCarthy

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An advertisement for Kalshi on a New York City subway train.

item.title: Maine Senate Polls Point to a Competitive General Election
item.description: Graham Platner is expected to win handily in the Democratic primary for Senate in Maine. The focus is shifting to how he might do in November.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler

media:content.media:credit: John Tully for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A polling place on Tuesday in Lewiston, Maine.

item.title: In Maine’s Primaries for Governor, Polling Is Sparse
item.description: The Democratic primary for governor is particularly competitive, but polls show that both party’s primaries could come down to ranked-choice voting.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters at the Augusta Civic Center in Augusta, Maine, on Tuesday.

item.title: Steve Hilton Wins Second Spot in California Governor Race, Beating Tom Steyer
item.description: Steve Hilton, a Republican endorsed by President Trump, will face Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, in the general election. Tom Steyer, another Democrat, will not advance.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Xavier Becerra, left, and Steve Hilton.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Paul LePage, Republican House Candidate in Maine
item.description: Mr. LePage served two terms as governor of Maine and was known for his incendiary rhetoric and his vetoing of legislation of all sorts.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson

media:content.media:credit: Taylor Coester/Reuters
media:content.media:description: “I was Donald Trump before Donald Trump became popular,” Paul Mr. LePage said in 2016, after endorsing Mr. Trump for president.

item.title: Jurors Balked at Indicting ICE Protesters. Prosecutors Kept Pushing.
item.description: Newly released transcripts showed a Chicago prosecutor repeatedly urging grand jurors to indict protesters of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman and Danny Hakim

media:content.media:credit: E. Jason Wambsgans/Chicago Tribune, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Andrew Boutros, the U.S. attorney in Chicago.

item.title: House defies Johnson and passes Democratic-led labor bill
item.description: Twenty G.O.P lawmakers broke with Speaker Mike Johnson and joined Democrats in backing a bill to amend the National Labor Relations Act.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The vote on Tuesday was the latest reflection of Speaker Mike Johnson’s weak hold on his narrow majority.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Graham Platner, Democratic Senate Candidate in Maine
item.description: Mr. Platner, an oyster farmer running for a Senate seat on a progressive platform, has drawn a large following among Democrats in Maine. His campaign has also surfaced damaging reports about his personal history.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner in April. He is expected to face Susan Collins, a five-term Republican senator, in the general election.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Susan Collins, Republican Senate Candidate in Maine
item.description: Ms. Collins, the longest-serving Republican woman in Senate history, is also her party’s most vulnerable incumbent this year.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Susan Collins of Maine has long cultivated a reputation for independence, but she has backed the Trump administration on some important votes.

item.title: Serena Williams Wins First Tennis Match in Nearly 4 Years
item.description: The 23-time Grand Slam singles champion and her doubles partner, Victoria Mboko, beat the No. 3 seeds at the Queen’s Club in west London on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Paul Harding/Getty Images

item.title: Doris Matsui to Face Mai Vang in General Election for Sacramento House Seat
item.description: Mai Vang, a progressive member of the Sacramento City Council, will challenge Ms. Matsui, the 81-year-old incumbent, in the general election.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Doris Matsui at the state Democratic convention in February.

item.title: Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases
item.description: A federal judge in Mississippi also imposed fines and canceled a civil trial, removing all four lawyers from the case.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:credit: Rogelio V. Solis/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: “This case presents the court with an unusual scenario — attorneys for both litigants engaged in similar sanctionable conduct,” wrote Judge Sharion Aycock.

item.title: Federal Judge Faces Battery Charge After Parking Lot Confrontation
item.description: Judge Ryan Nelson of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit tossed a man’s glasses and appeared to stomp on them during an argument in Idaho that was captured on video.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

item.title: What to Know About the Sea Drone That Rescued Downed Apache Crew
item.description: It was the first U.S. rescue carried out by an autonomous surface vessel and remotely piloted by a human operator, according to a military spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Taliban Leader Who Took U.S. Journalist Hostage Sentenced to 42 Years
item.description: Haji Najibullah had pleaded guilty to hostage taking and providing material support for terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Colin Moynihan

media:content.media:credit: Gary Cameron/Reuters
media:content.media:description: David Rohde was held captive in Pakistan for months after trying to speak to a Taliban leader for a book he was researching.

item.title: Air Canada Pilot Accused of Flying for 17 Years Without Proper License
item.description: The pilot, who retired last year before the investigation, held some valid flight credentials, officials said, but not the one required to be a captain.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman

media:content.media:credit: Sammy Kogan/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: An Air Canada pilot was the captain for 900 flights between 2009 and 2025 despite not having the proper license, Canadian officials said.
